prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='catboost', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.70) and extreme seasonality variance (1.06), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while catboost leads trend tracking and ARIMA excels at seasonality. The alternating true trend directions (down-up-down) combined with high disagreement strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_svr", "catboost", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6996,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.70) and extreme seasonality variance (1.06), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while catboost leads trend tracking and ARIMA excels at seasonality. The alternating true trend directions (down-up-down) combined with high disagreement strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.70 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.06 suggest DBA combination is optimal to handle phase shifts and non-linear divergence. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures the trend champions (catboost, ETS, THETA) while inverse_rmse_weights provides robust weighting across horizons."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses expanding window with past data only. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types). No redundant candidates. All names from valid_candidate_names. Score margin between top2 is small (0.00998) but acceptable.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.70) and extreme seasonality variance (1.06), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while catboost leads trend tracking and ARIMA excels at seasonality. The alternating true trend directions (down-up-down) combined with high disagreement strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.70 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.06 suggest DBA combination is optimal to handle phase shifts and non-linear divergence. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures the trend champions (catboost, ETS, THETA) while inverse_rmse_weights provides robust weighting across horizons.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses expanding window with past data only. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types). No redundant candidates. All names from valid_candidate_names. Score margin between top2 is small (0.00998) but acceptable.
